name: Romantic Comedies
description: The romantic comedy genre — landmark films, iconic scenes, and beloved couples

questions.q: Which 1990 rom-com starring Julia Roberts and Richard Gere follows a businessman and a sex worker in LA?

source.notes: Directed by Garry Marshall; launched Roberts to superstardom.

questions.q: Which 1989 Nora Ephron-scripted film poses the question of whether men and women can just be friends?

source.notes: Starring Billy Crystal and Meg Ryan; directed by Rob Reiner.

questions.q: Which 1999 romance film pairs Hugh Grant as a London bookshop owner with Julia Roberts's movie-star character?

source.notes: Written by Richard Curtis.

questions.q: Which Tom Hanks/Meg Ryan film is set around competing New York bookstores and an anonymous email romance?

source.notes: Remake of 1940's The Shop Around the Corner.

questions.q: Which 2003 Richard Curtis ensemble film interweaves London love stories in the weeks before Christmas?

source.notes: Features Hugh Grant, Emma Thompson, Liam Neeson, and a massive ensemble.

questions.q: In Jerry Maguire (1996), what is Renée Zellweger's character's famous reply to Tom Cruise's 'You complete me'?

source.notes: Directed and written by Cameron Crowe.

questions.q: Which 1987 cult classic is narrated as a bedtime story and features the line 'As you wish'?

source.notes: Directed by Rob Reiner; based on William Goldman's novel.

questions.q: Who wrote and directed You've Got Mail?

source.notes: Also wrote and directed Sleepless in Seattle.

questions.q: Which 1993 film has Meg Ryan falling for Tom Hanks after hearing him on a late-night radio show?

source.notes: Climax takes place at the Empire State Building, echoing An Affair to Remember.

questions.q: In the 1989 film Say Anything, which object does John Cusack's character hold aloft outside his love interest's window?

source.notes: Playing Peter Gabriel's 'In Your Eyes.'

questions.q: Which 1995 teen rom-com stars Alicia Silverstone as Cher Horowitz and is loosely based on Jane Austen's Emma?

source.notes: Written and directed by Amy Heckerling.

questions.q: Which 1999 teen rom-com, loosely based on Shakespeare's The Taming of the Shrew, stars Heath Ledger?

source.notes: Julia Stiles plays opposite Ledger.

questions.q: Which 2018 film adaptation of a Kevin Kwan novel became a landmark rom-com with an all-Asian cast?

source.notes: Directed by Jon M. Chu.

questions.q: Which 1994 Mike Newell-directed British comedy stars Hugh Grant and Andie MacDowell?

source.notes: Grant's breakthrough English-language role.

questions.q: Which 2009 Marc Webb film starring Joseph Gordon-Levitt and Zooey Deschanel is told out of chronological order?

source.notes: Subverts the traditional happy ending.

questions.q: Who stars as Bridget Jones in Bridget Jones's Diary (2001)?

source.notes: Oscar-nominated for Best Actress.

questions.q: Which 2012 David O. Russell rom-com earned Jennifer Lawrence her Best Actress Oscar?
choices: Seeking a Friend for the End of the World

source.notes: Pairs Lawrence with Bradley Cooper.

questions.q: The term 'meet-cute' refers to what in rom-com structure?

source.notes: A staple opening device for rom-coms.

questions.q: Which 1995 rom-com stars Sandra Bullock as an introverted Chicago transit employee who pretends to be the fiancée of a man in a coma?

source.notes: Bullock received a Golden Globe nomination.

questions.q: Which 2008 ABBA-soundtracked film features a bride-to-be inviting three potential fathers to her Greek island wedding?

source.notes: Based on the Broadway jukebox musical.

questions.q: Who directed the 1989 Billy Crystal/Meg Ryan rom-com When Harry Met Sally?

source.notes: Screenplay by Nora Ephron.

questions.q: Which 2004 Michel Gondry film about erasing memories of a lost love is a rom-com with a twist?
choices: Eternal Sunshine of the Spotless Mind

source.original_a: Eternal Sunshine of the Spotless Mind
source.notes: Charlie Kaufman won Best Original Screenplay.
